headline: Arunachal Pradesh holds the four lowest district CD ratios in India — Dibang Valley at 11.9%.
lede: Dibang Valley, Shi Yomi, Tawang and Upper Siang all sit under 21% — sparsely populated frontier hill districts where deposits accumulate from tourism and government salaries but viable lending opportunities are scarce. The state average is well below the RBI's 60% norm.
source: SOURCE · SLBC ARUNACHAL PRADESH · DEC 2025 · n = 25 DISTRICTS

headline: Rajasthan's Barmer runs a 340% CD ratio — banks lend three rupees for every one deposited.
lede: Outstanding advances of ₹26,107 crore dwarf deposits of just ₹7,684 crore. The pattern reflects large-ticket project lending — oil/gas, refineries, renewables — booked locally even though the depositor base remains small. Jodhpur and Kallakurichi (TN) follow the same shape.

headline: South 24 Parganas holds 56 lakh Jan Dhan accounts — more than 18 entire states.
lede: Three of the country's five largest PMJDY rolls are in West Bengal: South 24 Parganas (56.2 L), Murshidabad (51.7 L), Nadia (34.1 L). The Bengal lead reflects both population density and the depth of the rural BSBD push. Rajasthan's Jaipur (31.9 L) and West Bengal's Hooghly (26.5 L) round out the top five.

headline: 1-in-4 Jan Dhan accounts in Mizoram have never seen a single rupee.
lede: Lunglei (25.0%), Champhai (22.7%) and Kolasib (22.1%) in Mizoram top the zero-balance list, joined by Dibrugarh in Assam (22.4%) and Lower Siang in Arunachal (24.1%). Account opening succeeded; activation did not. By contrast, Noklak in Nagaland reports just 0.2% zero-balance — the cleanest activation rate in the country.

headline: West Bengal carries the heaviest KCC load — Hooghly tops at 3.95 lakh outstanding cards.
lede: Four of the country's six biggest KCC portfolios are in West Bengal: Hooghly, Purba Bardhaman (2.80 L), Birbhum (2.40 L), Bankura (1.98 L). Gujarat contributes Banas Kantha (3.17 L) and Rajkot (2.34 L). The clustering reflects the dense smallholder farming geography of the Gangetic plain and Saurashtra.
source: SOURCE · SLBC WEST BENGAL & GUJARAT · DEC 2025

headline: Aadhaar-seeding gap: Rajasthan runs 94%, Manipur barely 30%.
lede: In Barmer (RJ) 94.7% of operative savings accounts are Aadhaar-seeded — meaning they're DBT-ready. The same metric is 29.9% in Jiribam (Manipur), 30.8% in Tamenglong, 35.4% in Kamrup Metro (Assam). The seeding gap effectively rations access to Aadhaar-routed welfare in the hill districts.

headline: Pune district hosts 18,500 banking outlets — more than 200 districts combined.
lede: Pune (18,502) and Bengaluru Urban (17,641) anchor the top, followed by WB's North 24 Parganas, Murshidabad, and South 24 Parganas — each above 15,000. At the other extreme, Pakke Kessang (Arunachal) has just 3 outlets, Meluri in Nagaland has 5, and Kamjong in Manipur has 8.
source: SOURCE · RBI DBIE BANKING OUTLET LOCATOR · MAY 2026

headline: In Madhya Pradesh's Sidhi, there are 70 Business Correspondents for every bank branch.
lede: Nationally, 84% of formal banking outlets are non-branch — 168K branches against 2.08 million BCs and 215K CSPs. Sidhi (MP) takes this to the extreme: 74 branches, 5,220 BCs. Sheopur, Morena, Madhepura (Bihar) and Dhubri (Assam) all run 55–65× BC-to-branch ratios. In urban Delhi the ratio is below 1.
source: SOURCE · RBI DBIE BANKING OUTLET LOCATOR · MAY 2026

headline: Top-10 districts hold 26% of all India's capital-markets access; 65 districts have zero.
lede: Across 780 districts, the 10 largest — Mumbai Suburban (15,310 points), Thane, Pune, Ahmedabad, New Delhi, Bengaluru Urban, Surat, Kolkata and two more — together account for a quarter of all capital-markets service points. Capital markets reach remains aggressively urban.
source: SOURCE · CDSL + NSDL DP CENTRES + AMFI MFD LISTS · 2025

headline: Bengaluru Urban processed ₹15 lakh crore of UPI in a single quarter — more than 30 states combined.
statLabel: PHONEPE UPI · BENGALURU URBAN · Q4 FY24
lede: In the March 2024 quarter, PhonePe alone routed ₹14.98 lakh crore across 1.35 billion transactions inside Bengaluru Urban. PhonePe is roughly half the UPI market, so the underlying district volume is closer to ₹30 lakh crore. Pune, Rangareddy, Hyderabad and Jaipur follow, but none exceed half of Bengaluru's value.

headline: All eight Tripura districts now report 100% digital coverage of savings accounts.
statLabel: SB DIGITAL COVERAGE · ALL TRIPURA · SEP 2025
lede: Every operative savings account in Tripura is enabled with at least one digital payment mode (RuPay, UPI, IMPS, USSD or net banking). It's the only state in the SLBC dataset to hit that ceiling. At the other end, Khordha in Odisha sits at 70.6%, with Cuttack and Bhadrak only slightly higher.

headline: West Bengal's Murshidabad has 93,000 SHGs — more than 14 entire states' rolls.
lede: Murshidabad (92,933), South 24 Parganas (92,424), Purba Medinipur (80,484) and North 24 Parganas (74,395) are the largest SHG concentrations in India's NRLM MIS. The national total: 8.6 million SHGs covering 8.98 crore women. WB's SHG density is the result of 15 years of sustained Anandadhara mobilisation.

headline: Chennai has 2,147 bank branches; Kamle and Kamjong have one each.
lede: Chennai (2,147), Mumbai Suburban (1,450), Hyderabad (1,179), Thane (1,086), Surat (1,059). At the other end: Kamle (Arunachal) and Kamjong (Manipur), both newly carved districts, report a single branch. Anjaw, Dibang Valley, Kra Daadi report two. Branch reach remains one of the most spatially unequal pieces of Indian financial infrastructure.

headline: Rajasthan averages 88% health-insurance coverage; Bihar averages 14%.
lede: Rajasthan's state-mediated Chiranjeevi / MMHIS schemes pushed average district health-insurance coverage to 88.4% — the highest in India. Barmer (97.8%), Dungarpur (97.5%) and Jalore (96.6%) lead. The bottom is unambiguous: Bihar 14.1%, J&K 12.3%, North & Middle Andaman 2.1%.

headline: Meta's Relative Wealth Index ranks Central Delhi richest; Bijapur in Chhattisgarh poorest.
lede: The Chi et al. 2022 Relative Wealth Index (via SHRUG v2.1) places Central Delhi at +1.26 standard deviations above the Indian mean, with Chennai (+1.22), Kolkata (+1.18) and Mumbai (+1.15) close behind. Bijapur (Chhattisgarh) anchors the bottom at −0.53. State averages: Chandigarh leads, Mizoram tail-ends.
source: SOURCE · META/CHI ET AL. 2022 (CC BY-NC-SA) · VIA SHRUG v2.1

headline: Bihar's Sheohar saw nightlights brighten 30× between 2012 and 2023.
lede: VIIRS satellite mean radiance in Sheohar rose from 0.054 to 1.630 nW/cm²/sr over eleven years — a rural-electrification signature. Siwan (19×), Nawada (15×), Arwal (15×) and Sitamarhi (14×) follow; all top-5 growth districts are in Bihar. At the other end, North-East Delhi and East Delhi actually saw radiance fall — saturation + better light discipline.
source: SOURCE · NOAA/EOG VIIRS DNB · VIA SHRUG v2.1 (CC BY-NC-SA)

headline: Chhattisgarh's Surguja got 9,991 km of PMGSY rural roads — the most of any district in India.
lede: Medinipur West (WB, 7,763 km), Bankura (7,644 km), Birbhum (6,791 km) and Sidhi (MP, 6,214 km) follow. All leaders are eastern-belt high-density rural districts where village connectivity was the binding constraint at the start of the programme.
source: SOURCE · MORD OMMAS · VIA SHRUG v2.1 (CC BY-NC-SA)

headline: Some Assam districts report 19× more credit-linked SHGs than savings-linked groups — definitionally impossible.
lede: Five Assam districts — Charaideo, Karbi Anglong, Majuli, Chirang and Dhubri — report credit-linked SHG counts 15–19 times higher than savings-linked groups. Every credit-linked SHG must first be savings-linked, so the math is impossible. The likely cause: the SLBC report mixes cumulative credit-linked counts with current-quarter savings-linked counts.
source: SOURCE · SLBC ASSAM · SEP 2025 · FLAGGED IN DATA_VALIDATION_REPORT

headline: Bihar dominates the smallest MUDRA tier — Patna disbursed 78,000 Shishu loans in one quarter.
lede: Patna led the September 2025 quarter with 77,965 Shishu loans (sub-₹50,000 tickets), followed by Gaya (43,598), Muzaffarpur (29,936), Samastipur (23,686) — all in Bihar. By contrast, Jharkhand districts dominate the total MUDRA amount: Dhanbad (₹727.5 Cr), Ranchi (₹695.2 Cr), East Singhbhum (₹519.5 Cr).

headline: Maharashtra districts dominate PMSBY — Pune leads with 45 lakh insured.
lede: All five top districts for Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana — accident insurance at ₹20/year covering ₹2 lakh — are in Maharashtra: Pune (45.5 L), Thane (21.6 L), Mumbai Suburban (20.9 L), Nagpur (19.0 L), Nashik (18.5 L). The concentration reflects Maharashtra's aggressive bank-led auto-debit enrolment under the JDY co-pull.

headline: West Bengal's Murshidabad has the most APY pension subscribers of any district nationally.
lede: Atal Pension Yojana — the ₹1,000–5,000/month guaranteed pension for the unorganized sector — has its biggest district roll in Murshidabad (6.03 L), followed by Pune (5.49 L), Thane (5.32 L), South 24 Parganas (4.89 L), Jaipur (4.64 L). The Bengal lead is notable because APY enrolment correlates with formal-sector tracking; it suggests ASHA and SHG networks are pulling APY pickup.

headline: Rajasthan's Jodhpur disbursed ₹22 crore under PMEGP in a single quarter — the national peak.
lede: PMEGP — the Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme — peaked at ₹22.4 Cr in Jodhpur, followed by Jaipur (₹13.2 Cr), Imphal West (₹9.17 Cr), Aizawl (₹9.06 Cr), East Khasi Hills (₹7.67 Cr). The Rajasthan dominance is striking — the scheme typically over-indexes in the NE because of grant preferences, but Rajasthan's margin-money pipeline appears to have unblocked.

headline: Maharashtra's SC borrower disbursement is 5× the rest of the country combined.
lede: All five top districts for SC borrower loan disbursements are in Maharashtra: Pune (47,400), Nanded (41,400), Chandrapur (22,600), Thane (21,300), Nandurbar (19,200). No district outside Maharashtra appears in the top 30. The scale suggests MH is the only major state currently extracting and publishing this disaggregation at district level — a reporting-coverage finding as much as a substantive one.

headline: New Aadhaar enrolments Oct–Dec 2025: WB and Maharashtra cities lead, but children dominate.
statLabel: NEW AADHAAR · S 24 PARGANAS · Q3 FY26
lede: The highest new-Aadhaar district counts in Oct–Dec 2025 come from South 24 Parganas (24,083), Murshidabad (22,034), Pune (17,607), Thane (17,331), Jaipur (14,943) and Hyderabad (14,710). Children aged 0–5 dominate the enrolments in Murshidabad (19,172) and South 24 Parganas (18,060) — first-time Aadhaar issuance for newborns, not adult re-enrolment.
source: SOURCE · UIDAI AADHAAR ENROLMENT HACKATHON 2026 DATASET

headline: Banking the unbanked is 84% non-branch: India runs 2.08 million BCs against 168,000 branches.
lede: Aggregating the RBI DBIE locator: India's formal banking footprint is 168K branches, 2.08 million Business Correspondents, 215K Customer Service Points — 2.47 million total outlets. 67% sit in rural areas; only 8% in metros. The choropleth-level density patterns trace the BC network, not the branch network, in every rural state.
source: SOURCE · RBI DBIE BANKING OUTLET LOCATOR · MAY 2026
